title: The judge of the 2016 Nice terror attack trial reflects on 'abominable' case, 10 years after
subhed: Laurent Raviot presided over the Paris special criminal court that heard the trial of the attack in Nice that killed 86 people on July 14, 2016. On the 10th anniversary of the attack, he granted Le Monde his first interview, speaking with unusual candor.

title: France celebrates Bastille Day, Macron's last as president, with parade showcasing unity with allies and Ukraine
Spain stun France to reach World Cup 2026 final
Ukraine expands attacks to the Sea of Azov, a crucial maritime corridor for Russia
subhed: After choking off Crimea, which Moscow has occupied since 2014, Ukrainian drones are waging a relentless naval campaign against Russia's so-called 'shadow fleet,' which has been accused of evading sanctions. This has brought the traffic of these vessels on the Sea of Azov to a near halt.

title: The International Criminal Court targeted by a new US offensive
subhed: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reopened a diplomatic campaign on Monday aimed at dismantling the ICC by combining sanctions, putting pressure on member states and seeking allies among major powers hostile to the institution.

title: In Fontainebleau, southern France firefighters share expertise with Paris-region colleagues
subhed: A total of 2,050 hectares or 10% of vegetation has burned in the Fontainebleau forest southeast of the capital. The rare northern blaze saw the deployment of seasoned firefighters from the Mediterranean region, as well as Canadair water bombers.

title: 'Les Bleus represent France because France entrusted them with its place and they accepted it'
subhed: As players of the French national football team are targeted by racist remarks, political scientist and writer Rachid Benzine argues that confusion has arisen over what representation means, contending that it is not the same as resemblance.

Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
subhed: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

title: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhed: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.

title: 'And I thought coke was for stressed-out executives!': Cocaine's ravages among manual workers
subhed: 'Cocaine at work' (3/3). Long associated with elite, urban circles, cocaine has now made its way deep into rural France and its mid-sized towns. On construction sites, farms and in warehouses, workers, some barely out of their teens, are turning to the drug to get through the physical toll of their jobs.
